title: Rash

red_flags.text: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.
red_flags.rationale: Reviewed to enforce the pediatric population boundary; no content was mapped from scope alone.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines pediatric rash use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

red_flags.text: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped pediatric fever, pain, immune compromise, uncertainty, worsening, return, and follow-up prompts.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists emergency and face-to-face review criteria, including confusion, stiff neck, photophobia, persistent or recurrent rash, fever, immunocompromise, and diagnostic uncertainty.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

red_flags.text: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped fever, painful or rapidly worsening eczema, blister, and infection-warning fields.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

assessment.text: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.
assessment.rationale: Supports mapped eczema or dermatitis terminology and compatible itch, scale, vesicle, excoriation, and erythema fields.
assessment.evidence_extract: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

medication.text: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped pediatric rash history, distribution, morphology, symptom, exposure, medication, impact, and observed skin fields.
medication.evidence_extract: Specifies visual assessment plus duration, evolution, distribution, symptoms, family history, recurrence, exposures, medication, impact, travel, allergy, and pet history.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

medication.text: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.
medication.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-documented skin care, trigger, medicine, education, and question fields.
medication.evidence_extract: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Atopic Dermatitis

follow_up.text: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.
follow_up.rationale: Reviewed to understand fever and itching groupings without assigning a diagnosis; no content was mapped from grouping alone.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Organises pediatric rashes by fever, itching, and neither fever nor itching without proving a diagnosis for an individual child.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Rashes in Children – 49

safety_netting.text: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.
safety_netting.rationale: Supports mapped breathing and facial or lip swelling warnings only; skin findings alone are explicitly not treated as anaphylaxis.
safety_netting.evidence_extract: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers

source_limitations: Adult undifferentiated rash, mucosal involvement, drug eruptions, generalized blistering or peeling, sick contacts, wound fields, bloods, swabs, fixed one-week timing, and many diagnosis-specific treatments remain unsupported.
Contact dermatitis or eczema remains clinician-stated and the broad workflow must not infer anaphylaxis from skin findings alone.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
